reason: Defines local settings, microphone/output device, and free-talk vocabulary and MVP exclusions.
Locks Wails 3 as the Windows shell boundary for window sizing and WebView2.
Assigns device discovery and switching to WebView2/LiveKit JS rather than Go audio code.
Preserves Wails/Go ownership of saved local device preferences.
Provides cold-light visual tokens and desktop window/layout constraints.
Guides the WebView device adapter, generated binding, and UI error-state boundaries.
Task-specific requirements, exclusions, and acceptance criteria.
Approved technical design and rollback boundary.
